_comment: TEMPLATE — /delegation-setup replaces every <placeholder> with live model IDs from mcp__paseo__list_models. Role categories are the ones all paseo skills read: impl, ui, research, planning, audit.
preferences: Route by tier, not habit: the Claude workhorse is the implementation default; the OpenAI workhorse is the default contrarian reviewer and design challenger.
Escalate to claude/<claude-frontier-id> ONLY when a problem already resisted a first attempt or is clearly gnarly architecture. Never for routine work — it is the most expensive tier.
Escalate to codex/<openai-frontier-id> (thinking xhigh) for hard contrarian analysis, high-stakes reviews, or when a review is repeatedly inconclusive. Ordinary reviews stay on the OpenAI workhorse.
Cross-family pairing: work authored by a Claude agent gets reviewed by a Codex agent, and vice versa. Frontier-vs-frontier only to settle hard disagreements.
Downshift to claude/<claude-trivial-id> or codex/<openai-trivial-id> for trivial mechanical work: test scaffolding, renames, format-only changes, log spelunking.
Always pass an explicit provider to create_agent. Never let an agent inherit or default its model.
Before spawning, state the chosen tier and a one-line reason (task difficulty class + role).
